title: Failing DNS inside compose?
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue:
Caddy admin is failing to connect/setup not sure why. If you set admin off then it works but if you do this you cannot reload the caddyfile.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Copy/Re-create the docker-compose and Dockerfile
2. 


### Describe the results you received

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details
CachyOS
Not inside a container / vm
podman-compose is used as the compose provider. Likely not an issue with that though, same issue was recreated with docker-compose. 

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting.

Caddyfile
==
```
{
    frankenphp
    # admin off
}

# in theory can reproduce issue even without this block
http://localhost:8080 {
    root * /app
    encode gzip

    # php_server

    file_server browse
}
```

docker-compose.yaml
==
```
services:
  app:
    image: localhost/practical-exam_app:latest
    container_name: app
    volumes:
      - ./Caddyfile:/etc/frankenphp/Caddyfile
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
```

Dockerfile
==
```
FROM docker.io/serversideup/php-dev:645-frankenphp-alpine

USER root

RUN install-php-extensions \
    pdo_mysql \
    mysqli

USER www-data
```

comments.body:
I don't know Caddy and I do not see any admin option documented there but

>  [app] | Error: loading initial config: loading new config: starting caddy administration endpoint: listen tcp: lookup off on 10.89.0.1:53: no such host

That error seems to suggest "off" is being looked up as hostname and our dns server simply responds that this name does not exists which all sounds good and not at all like a podman problem.

Apologies. There is no issue with podman. 

I have done a more thorough check and found the issue to be in the docker image: docker.io/serversideup/php-dev:645-frankenphp-alpine where they are turning off the caddy admin endpoint incorrectly through CADDY_ADMIN=off env var. This results in caddy assuming the admin is running as a separate process on a remote and doing a dns lookup for "off". This issue exists in docker.io/serversideup/php-dev:645-frankenphp-alpine and docker.io/serversideup/php:8.3-frankenphp at the very least. Also when I say i tried turning admin off and testing and it was fine, what i mean is through caddyfile setting admin off is supported behaviour and is the proper method of turning off the admin server. Im not sure if i mentioned this but frankenphp is a modified image of caddy, but serversideup's image is a modified version of frankenphp.

Sadly I do not know how to report this issue to the image publishers, any ideas on this?

Here is docs on caddy admin if your curious: ctrl + f and search admin. Typically caddy hosts an admin REST server on same machine so i assumed it failed to find it. Interestingly setting admin :2019 manually through solves this as it manually overrides the incorrect env var.
https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/options#general-options

> Sadly I do not know how to report this issue to the image publishers, any ideas on this?

```
$ skopeo inspect --config docker://docker.io/serversideup/php:8.3-frankenphp
        "Labels": {
            "org.opencontainers.image.authors": "Jay Rogers (@jaydrogers)",
            "org.opencontainers.image.description": "Supercharge your PHP experience. Based off the official PHP images, serversideup/php includes pre-configured PHP extensions and settings for enhanced performance and security. Optimized for Laravel and WordPress.",
            "org.opencontainers.image.documentation": "https://serversideup.net/open-source/docker-php/docs/",
            "org.opencontainers.image.licenses": "GPL-3.0-or-later",
            "org.opencontainers.image.source": "https://github.com/serversideup/docker-php",
            "org.opencontainers.image.title": "serversideup/php (frankenphp)",
            "org.opencontainers.image.url": "https://serversideup.net/open-source/docker-php/",
            "org.opencontainers.image.vendor": "ServerSideUp",
            "org.opencontainers.image.version": "v4.4.1-28015645414"
        },
```

Or well since you have the image locally podman image inspect should show you the same labels. This shows a repo link and a author name.
